paper.title: Universal Barcoding Predicts In Vivo ApoE-Independent Lipid Nanoparticle Delivery
paper.authors: Da Silva Sanchez AJ et al.

formulations.targeting_details: No active targeting. Library includes 3-component LNPs without helper lipid. Endogenous targeting via protein corona.
formulations.formulation_notes: 98 chemically distinct LNPs formulated with DNA barcodes and siRNA. Universal barcoding allows testing in any mouse model. Stereopure (R vs S) C12-200 variants included.

formulations.targeting_details: ApoE-independent delivery to Kupffer cells, endothelial cells, and hepatocytes. Novel stereopure LNP identified from screen.
formulations.formulation_notes: Lead ApoE-independent LNP identified from the 98-LNP screen. Targets liver cells via non-ApoE mechanism. Stereopure (specific stereoisomer) version of C12-200.

results.liver_metric: siRNA-mediated protein knockdown in 11 cell types
results.liver_notes: 11 cell types measured across multiple organs. Liver cell types: hepatocytes, Kupffer cells, endothelial cells. Focus is on functional delivery (protein knockdown), not biodistribution.

experiments.notes: 98 LNPs tested in WT mice. 11 cell types quantified. Functional siRNA knockdown readout. Per-formulation data behind paywall.

results.liver_metric: siRNA knockdown in ApoE-/- vs WT
results.liver_notes: Key finding: identified novel stereopure LNP that delivers to Kupffer cells, endothelial cells, and hepatocytes ApoE-independently.

experiments.notes: Same 98 LNPs tested in ApoE-/- mice. Also tested in LDLR-/- and VLDLR-/- mice. Universal barcoding enables cross-genotype comparison.

key_findings_for_project: Establishes that ApoE-independent delivery mechanisms EXIST for LNPs
98 LNPs profiled across 4 mouse genotypes (WT, ApoE-/-, LDLR-/-, VLDLR-/-) and 11 cell types
Stereopure C12-200 variant identified as ApoE-independent - stereochemistry matters for tropism
Universal barcoding methodology applicable to any knockout model
BM cell types NOT explicitly reported in abstract - focus is liver cell types
Dahlman lab - compatible methodology with Kim 2024 and Gentry 2025 datasets
Critical for understanding translation: human ApoE-LNP interactions may differ from mouse

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Breda used CD117-targeted tLNPs (ApoE role unclear). Da Silva Sanchez data could inform whether Breda's delivery is ApoE-dependent.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Shi/Anderson used CD117-targeted tLNPs. ApoE independence of targeted LNPs is an open question.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Kim used PPZ-A10 untargeted LNPs. Kim's system in ApoE-KO context unknown. Da Silva Sanchez's barcoding approach is the predecessor methodology to Kim's screen.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: No BM data, no Pareto placement possible.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Indirect value: understanding ApoE dependence informs which formulations will translate to human patients.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: 3-component LNPs (no helper) mentioned in user notes. If validated, this simplifies formulation space. C12-200 has known SMILES.

limitations_and_caveats: PAYWALL: Quantitative per-formulation delivery data behind ACS paywall
BM cell types not mentioned in abstract - unclear if BM was measured at all
siRNA readout (knockdown) rather than mRNA delivery - different pharmacology
C12-200 is known to have tolerability issues - not clinically viable base lipid
3-component LNP claim (from user notes) needs verification from full paper
11 cell types measured but cell type identities only partially listed in abstract
